an1.21:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that is so unworkable when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.21:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*], is unworkable."
an1.22:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that is so workable when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.22:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*], is workable."
an1.23:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*], leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.23:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*], leads to great harm."
an1.24:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*], leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.24:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*], leads to great benefit."
an1.25:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] and not made manifest, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.25:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] and not made manifest, leads to great harm."
an1.26:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] and made manifest, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.26:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] and made manifest, leads to great benefit."
an1.27:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] and not frequently cultivated, leads to such great harm as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.27:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] and not frequently cultivated, leads to great harm."
an1.28:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] and frequently cultivated, leads to such great benefit as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.28:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] and frequently cultivated, leads to great benefit."
an1.29:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] and not frequently cultivated, brings such *dukkha* as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.29:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when undeveloped [*abhāvitaṁ*] and not frequently cultivated, brings *dukkha*."
an1.30:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] and frequently cultivated, brings such *sukha* as this: the mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*.
an1.30:1.2: The mind [*citta*], *bhikkhus*, when developed [*bhāvitaṁ*] and frequently cultivated, brings *sukha*."
an1.30:1.4: The Chapter on the Unworkable, the third.
